heading: 1 Quadratics
text:
quadratic expression (one variable \(x\)) expression can written form \(ax^2 + bx + c\), \(, b, c\) numbers \(\neq 0\). root \(ax^2 + bx + c\) value \(x\) expression evaluates \(0\). , root \(ax^2 + bx + c\) solution equation
\[ax^2 + bx + c = 0.\]

heading: 1.1 Finding roots by factoring
text: cases, quadratic factors nicely product two linear expressions (perhaps constant), case roots can found looking linear factor individually.Example: \(x^2 - 7x + 12\) factors \((x - 3)(x - 4)\). Therefore, \(x^2 - 7x + 12 = 0\) \(x - 3 = 0\) \(x - 4 = 0\), roots \(3\) \(4\).Example: \(x^2 + 6x + 9\) factors \((x + 3)^2\), root \(-3\).Example: \(10x^2 + 17x + 3\) factors \((2x + 3)(5x + 1)\), \(10x^2 + 17x + 3 = 0\) \(2x + 3 = 0\) \(5x + 1 = 0\). roots thus \(-3/2\) \(-1/5\).Conversely, know number \(r\) root quadratic, \(x - r\) must factor.Example: One roots \(6x^2 - 5x - 1\) \(1\), \(x - 1\) factor. Choosing coefficients factor match quadratic term \(6x^2\) constant term \(-1\), see \(6x^2 - 5x - 1\) factors \((x - 1)(6x + 1)\). root \(-1/6\).multiplicity root \(r\) number times \(x - r\) appears factor.Example: \(x^2 - 7x + 12 = (x - 3)(x - 4)\), roots \(3\) \(4\) multiplicity \(1\). hand, \(x^2 + 6x + 9 = (x + 3)^2\), lone root \(-3\) multiplicity \(2\).

heading: 1.1.1 Factoring quadratics which are not monic
text:
quadratic monic leading coefficient, .e. coefficient \(x^2\), \(1\). generally easier factor (nice factorisation exists), decide constant terms linear factors. non-monic quadratics, following result helpful.Proposition: roots \(x^2 + bx + ac\) \(\) times roots \(ax^2 + bx + c\).Proof: Let \(r\) root \(ax^2 + bx + c\), \(ar^2 + br + c = 0\). 
\[(ar)^2 + b(ar) + ac = (ar^2 + br + c) = \cdot 0 = 0,\]
\(ar\) root \(x^2 + bx + ac\). Conversely, \(s\) root \(x^2 + bx + ac\), 
\[(s/)^2 + b(s/) + c = (1/)(s^2 + bs + ac) = (1/)\cdot 0 = 0,\]
\(s/\) root \(ax^2 + bx + c\).Corollary: \(x^2 + bx + ac = (x - r)(x - s)\), \(ax^2 + bx + c = (x - r/)(x - s/)\).Example: Returning earlier example \(10x^2 + 17x + 3\), Proposition, can start factoring \(x^2 + 17x + 30 = (x + 2)(x + 15)\). 
\[10x^2 + 17x + 3 = 10\left(x + \frac{2}{10}\right)\left(x + \frac{15}{10}\right) = (5x + 1)(2x + 3),\]
claimed .

heading: 1.1.2 Useful factorisations
text:
factorisations useful either recognise create:
\[\begin{aligned}
x^2 - ^2 &= (x - )(x + ) \\
x^2 + 2ax + ^2 &= (x + )^2 \\
x^2 - 2ax + ^2 &= (x - )^2.
\end{aligned}\]

heading: 2 Cross-references
text: Cross-references make easier readers find link elements book.

heading: 2.1 Chapters and sub-chapters
text:
two steps cross-reference heading:Label heading: # Hello world {#nice-label}.
Leave label like automated heading generated based heading title: example, # Hello world = # Hello world {#hello-world}.
label un-numbered heading, use: # Hello world {-#nice-label} {# Hello world .unnumbered}.
Leave label like automated heading generated based heading title: example, # Hello world = # Hello world {#hello-world}.label un-numbered heading, use: # Hello world {-#nice-label} {# Hello world .unnumbered}.Next, reference labeled heading anywhere text using \@ref(nice-label); example, please see Chapter 2.
prefer text link instead numbered reference use: text want can go .
prefer text link instead numbered reference use: text want can go .

heading: 2.2 Captioned figures and tables
text:
Figures tables captions can also cross-referenced elsewhere book using \@ref(fig:chunk-label) \@ref(tab:chunk-label), respectively.See Figure 2.1.
Figure 2.1: nice figure!
Don’t miss Table 2.1.Table 2.1: nice table!
code:
par(mar = c(4, 4, .1, .1))
plot(pressure, type = 'b', pch = 19)
knitr::kable(
  head(pressure, 10), caption = 'Here is a nice table!',
  booktabs = TRUE
)

heading: 3 Parts
text: can add parts organize one book chapters together. Parts can inserted top .Rmd file, first-level chapter heading file.Add numbered part: # (PART) Act one {-} (followed # chapter)Add unnumbered part: # (PART\*) Act one {-} (followed # chapter)Add appendix special kind un-numbered part: # (APPENDIX) stuff {-} (followed # chapter). Chapters appendix prepended letters instead numbers.

heading: 4.1 Footnotes
text: Footnotes put inside square brackets caret ^[]. Like one 1.

heading: 4.2 Citations
text:
Reference items bibliography file(s) using @key.example, using bookdown package2 (check last code chunk index.Rmd see citation key added) sample book, built top R Markdown knitr3 (citation added manually external file book.bib).
Note .bib files need listed index.Rmd YAML bibliography key.bs4_book theme makes footnotes appear inline click . example book, added csl: chicago-fullnote-bibliography.csl index.Rmd YAML, include .csl file. download new style, recommend: https://www.zotero.org/styles/RStudio Visual Markdown Editor can also make easier insert citations: https://rstudio.github.io/visual-markdown-editing/#/citations
